question.text: Loss on derivative transactions carried out on a recognized stock exchange can be set off against any income during the year EXCEPT:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Derivative losses from recognized exchanges are treated as non-speculative business loss. Such losses can be set off against any other income EXCEPT salary income during the assessment year.
**The Trap:** Derivative losses CAN be set off against business income, income from other sources, and capital gains — just not salary. The restriction on salary income is a general tax principle.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Tax set-off rules for derivative losses are tested in Series IV and are important for traders managing tax liabilities across income categories.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If derivative losses cannot be fully set off in the current year, the remaining loss can be carried forward for up to 8 assessment years.
